scenario.context: Station 05 of the day run: apply compression, encryption, and hashing.
scenario.mission: You distinguish the methods cleanly and use them appropriately for integrity, confidentiality, and efficiency.

customerRequest.subject: Station 05: Protection and integrity logic
customerRequest.body: Please design how compression, encryption, and hashing should be used for the module.

feedback.correct: Correct: Protection goals and methods are separated correctly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: Please separate integrity, confidentiality, and efficiency more cleanly.

questions.title: Choose combination
questions.prompt: Which combination makes sense for secure data transfer?

options.text: Compress data, encrypt it, and verify integrity with a hash

feedback.correct: Correct: The combination matches the protection goal.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: Keep the full protection chain in mind.

questions.title: Order the secure flow
questions.prompt: Put the steps for secure data transfer in order.

feedback.wrong: Not ideal: Keep validation, protection, and verification in the correct order.

questions.title: Data reduction in percent
questions.prompt: File size drops from 950 KB to 665 KB. What is the reduction in percent (rounded)?

feedback.wrong: Please relate the reduction correctly to the original size.

questions.title: Note the crypto decision
questions.prompt: Describe in 4-6 sentences how you would combine compression, encryption, and hashing in this case.
questions.expectedOutline: Combination logic with a clear purpose for each method.

feedback.correct: Correct: The security logic is traceable.
feedback.wrong: Please separate the roles of the three methods more clearly.
